suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: When scrutiny reruns after fix features land, reviewer prompts and synthesis guidance should explicitly distinguish original failed commits from later fix features so legacy feature reports are not treated as current milestone failures once the fix features have been reviewed and passed.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: This rerun reviewed both the original m2 features and the later fix features. The original reports remained fail against historical commits 05ee508, while focused fix reviews on f596cf45 and 565419e proved VAL-NS-008 and VAL-CROSS-002 are now satisfied on HEAD.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify the expected GitNexus fallback wording for worker handoffs when CLI commands like detect_changes are unavailable, so followedProcedure reporting stays consistent across workers and reviewers.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Multiple review reports noted that current repo guidance requires GitNexus-first workflows but the available CLI did not expose detect_changes, causing workers to fall back to git/jj inspection with inconsistent reporting style.

rejectedObservations.reason: Useful knowledge, but not required to complete scrutiny and not necessary to apply during this synthesis pass.

rejectedObservations.observation: Record in shared knowledge that the original 05ee508 commit did not yet prove metadata and nested override contracts.
rejectedObservations.reason: The rerun synthesis already captures that historical distinction via previousRound plus fix-feature review outcomes; duplicating it into library state would be redundant.
